page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 15 GB of system memory, yielding 7.5 GB of RAM per vCPU. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The underlying hardware configuration consists of 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2229.0 and high single-threaded compression throughput, with gzip compression reaching 34.51 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are significantly lower. The stress-ng multi-core benchmark falls into the bottom 10% with a score of 1960.58, and uncached memory write bandwidth is limited to 12.28 GB/sec. Advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) are available to accelerate specialized vector and matrix operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on dedicated Intel Xeon hardware, making it a viable option for workloads requiring more memory capacity than raw multi-core compute power. Given its strong single-threaded performance but constrained multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, this instance is best suited for lightweight database operations, single-threaded text processing, basic web serving, and small-scale memory-bound applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 1 physical core with 2 threads and a 260 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show exceptional single-core performance, with top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores, though multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth remain relatively weak. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) for specialized workloads. Qualitatively, this instance offers a high memory-to-core ratio, making it cost-effective for memory-leaning, single-threaded tasks. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, text processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring moderate memory without intensive multi-core parallel processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It offers strong single-core performance for memory-leaning workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and strong single-threaded performance for memory-leaning workloads.

Strong single-core performance with top-tier single-threaded compression
Equipped with Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight databases, text processing, and memory-leaning tasks
